pageTitle: Life Compass — We are with you

hero.title: You are not alone
hero.subtitle: Behind every number is a person, a family, a story. We are here to walk with you. There is always another way.
hero.crisisMessage: If you or someone you know is going through a difficult time, there are people who want to listen. Call, write, don't stay silent.
inspirational: This pain is not the end.
One more day can change everything.
Breathe. One moment at a time.
You don't have to give up today.
There are still paths to discover.
It's okay to ask for help.
You are stronger than you think.
Give us one more day of you.
Your silence doesn't have to last forever.

globalStats.title: Global Overview
globalStats.subtitle: Understanding the scale helps us take meaningful action.
globalStats.mostAffectedAgeDesc: Second leading cause of death in this range

gender.title: Gender Analysis
gender.subtitle: Understanding gender differences is crucial for targeted prevention.
gender.insight1: Men account for ~75% of suicides in high-income countries
gender.insight2: Women have higher attempt rates but lower fatality

economic.title: Economic Factors
economic.subtitle: Economic stability plays a critical role in mental health outcomes.
economic.insight1: Economic crises increase suicide risk by 20–30%
economic.insight2: Job loss is a leading risk factor, especially in men 40–60
economic.insight3: Poverty and debt are significant contributing factors
economic.insight4: Countries with stronger social safety nets show lower rates
economic.gdpVsRate: GDP per Capita vs Suicide Rate
economic.unemploymentVsRate: Unemployment vs Suicide Rate Over Time

social.title: Social Media & Digital Impact
social.subtitle: The digital world creates both risks and opportunities for mental health.
social.stat1Text: higher risk in teens with >5hrs/day screen time
social.stat3Text: of Gen Z say social media negatively affects mental health
social.chartTitle: Screen Time vs Suicide Ideation by Age

ageGroups.title: Age Group Deep Dive
ageGroups.subtitle: Risk factors change across the lifespan — prevention must adapt accordingly.
ageGroups.chartTitle: Suicide Rates by Age Group Over Time

charts.title: Data & Trends
charts.subtitle: The global trend is declining — a sign that prevention works.

helplines.title: Helpline Directory
helplines.subtitle: Help is available in your country. You are not alone.

prevention.title: Prevention & Resources
prevention.subtitle: Knowledge saves lives. Here's how you can help.
prevention.warning1: Talking about wanting to die or feeling hopeless
prevention.warning2: Withdrawing from friends, family, and activities
prevention.warning3: Dramatic mood changes or extreme anxiety
prevention.warning5: Sleeping too much or too little
prevention.step1Desc: "Are you thinking about suicide?" — asking doesn't increase risk.
prevention.step2Desc: Be present. Don't try to fix — just be there.
prevention.step3Desc: Remove access to means. Stay with them.
prevention.step4Desc: Guide them to professional help or a crisis line.
prevention.step5Desc: Check in regularly. Your continued presence matters.
prevention.doSay1: "I'm here for you, and I care."
prevention.dontSay1Better: "I hear you. Your feelings are valid."
prevention.dontSay3Better: "I can see you're in a lot of pain."

emergency.messageFull: Need help now? You are not alone. Help is available 24/7.

footer.crisisMessage: If you or someone you know is in crisis, please reach out. Help is available 24/7.
footer.builtWithCare: Built with care. Every life is a story worth continuing.
